generated_oml:

    base:desc "Centralized hub for bidirectional data exchange in Digital Twin framework"
]

    base:desc "Role responsible for device modeling in Building Control System"
]

    base:desc "Role for real-time monitoring and control of infrastructure"
]

]

instance AECO_DigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "AECO domain environment for building lifecycle management"

]

instance Building_System : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Cyber-physical information construct representing a building asset in AECO domain for lifecycle management"
]

instance BIM_Model : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Building Information Modeling (BIM) as geometric foundation for digital twin"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MDE_Enabler
]

    base:desc "Behavioral models for Building Control System components including sensors, actuators, and controllers"

]

]

]

]

instance SensorTelemetry : DTDFVocab:Data [

]

]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control signals are transmitted from the digital twin to Building Control Systems (BCS) via runtime interfaces, enabling Facility Managers to operate physical infrastructure. Bidirectional communication mechanisms exist for control feedback, though command formats, validation, conflict resolution, and execution confirmation details are not specified in the document."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "Real-time data exchange capabilities ensure continuous synchronization between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, though specific synchronization frequencies, latency requirements, and temporal granularity details are not provided in the document."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT Framework serves as a central hub orchestrating bidirectional data exchange across roles including Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, and DT Engineers. Modular architecture with standardized extension points enables component integration, while runtime configuration management supports adaptability for holistic building lifecycle management."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin lifecycle encompasses initial device modeling within BIM environments by Building Control Engineers, followed by Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) transformation to extract structured representations and automatically generate deployable artifacts. Subsequent phases include real-time data synchronization with physical infrastructure, interactive visualization for monitoring and control by Facility Managers, and extensibility through custom services developed by DT Engineers. Transitions between lifecycle stages are managed via automated MDE processes with minimal manual intervention, supported by role-based governance structures."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Eclipse Hono and Ditto middleware enable real-time data exchange with standardized metadata handling, ensuring interoperability across heterogeneous systems. Specific network topology, bandwidth, latency, reliability, and security protocols are not detailed in the document."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "Model-driven deployment with automated processes minimizes manual intervention, leveraging modular architecture and standardized extension points for scalable deployment. Runtime configuration management enables dynamic adjustments without full redeployment, supporting scalability from individual components to entire infrastructure systems."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Interoperability across heterogeneous systems is addressed via nonfunctional requirements (NF_X), with BIM tools utilized for device modeling by Building Control Engineers; however, specific data exchange protocols, external system dependencies, or integration patterns with other Digital Twins or enterprise systems are not detailed in the document."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "Standardization is implemented through standardized extension points in the modular architecture to ensure interoperability for custom service integration. While BIM models are utilized as a foundational element, their limitations for seamless DT deployment are acknowledged. However, no specific industry standards (e.g., ISO, IEC), compliance frameworks, or certification requirements are detailed in the provided documents."
]
